tasks.title: Fix pre-existing musicgen/audiocraft test failures in tests/test_music.py
tasks.description:
4 tests in tests/test_music.py fail with 'No module named audiocraft.models.musicgen' on the CI/main environment. These failures pre-date any current task branches and block ALL merge queue submissions — no branch can land until this is resolved.

Tests failing:
- 4 tests in TestMusicGen class (approximate)
- Error: `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'audiocraft.models.musicgen'`

Audiocraft/musicgen is an optional heavy ML dependency not available in the test environment.

## Fix options (in order of preference)
1. **Skip with pytest.importorskip**: Add `audiocraft = pytest.importorskip('audiocraft.models.musicgen')` at top of test class or each test — skips gracefully when module is absent.
2. **Mock the import**: Use `unittest.mock.patch.dict('sys.modules', {'audiocraft': MagicMock(), 'audiocraft.models': MagicMock(), 'audiocraft.models.musicgen': MagicMock()})` as a session-scoped fixture.
3. **Conditional skip marker**: `@pytest.mark.skipif(not importlib.util.find_spec('audiocraft'), reason='audiocraft not installed')` on the class.

Option 1 (importorskip) is preferred — it's idiomatic pytest and makes the skip reason explicit in the test report.

## Memory hints
- search: 'audiocraft musicgen test failures merge queue blocker'
- search: 'pytest.importorskip optional ML dependency skip pattern'

tasks.title: Emit notification events from orchestrator to wire up bell badge
tasks.description:
The notification bell badge in the web console is fully wired client-side (app.js line 35 parses data.message/data.type → showToast(); updateNotificationBadge toggles #notification-badge hidden class), but the orchestrator never emits any SSE events with type='notification'. The bell is dead end-to-end despite the client handler existing.

## What's needed
The orchestrator (or a server-side pipeline event emitter) must write rows to the pipeline_events SQLite table with event_type='notification' at appropriate pipeline milestones. The SSE endpoint polls this table and streams matching events to connected clients.

## Candidate notification points
- Gate reached (already emitted as gate_waiting) — may also emit a notification variant
- Run completed / run failed — high-value user-facing events
- Stage error — surfaces unexpected failures to the user immediately
- Upload finished — confirms YouTube upload succeeded

## Implementation approach
1. Identify where gate_waiting, run_completed, run_failed, stage_error are inserted into pipeline_events (orchestrator.py or a shared events helper).
2. For each, add a companion INSERT with event_type='notification', data={'message': '...', 'type': 'info'|'warning'|'error'}.
3. Verify app.js showToast() receives the message and the badge lights up.
4. Add tests: one per notification event type, asserting the correct message and type fields.

## Memory hints
- search: 'pipeline_events table SSE orchestrator INSERT event_type'
- search: 'notification bell badge updateNotificationBadge app.js'
- entities: pipeline_events, orchestrator.py, app.js, updateNotificationBadge

tasks.title: Fix DB migration: use PRAGMA table_info and eliminate per-request overhead
tasks.description:
In autopilot/db.py around lines 268-273, the ALTER TABLE migration (1) runs on every CatalogDB.__init__ call (every HTTP request), paying the cost of a failed DDL each time, and (2) catches all sqlite3.OperationalError, silently swallowing non-duplicate-column errors like disk-full or locking failures.

Fix: check `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pragma_table_info('activity_clusters') WHERE name='excluded'` once (at app startup or lazily via a class-level flag) and only issue ALTER TABLE when the column is absent. Also add a brief comment about DDL auto-commit behavior.

tasks.title: Improve api_stream_video: dynamic MIME type and TOCTOU safety
tasks.description:
In autopilot/web/routes/review.py around line 544, the FileResponse always uses media_type='video/mp4'. If the pipeline produces .mov or .webm the browser gets a wrong Content-Type. Also, the Path.is_file() check followed by FileResponse construction is non-atomic — a deleted file between check and open returns a 500 instead of a 404.

Fix: derive media type from the extension using `mimetypes.guess_type(render_path)[0] or 'video/mp4'`, and wrap the FileResponse construction in try/except OSError, re-raising as HTTPException(404).

tasks.title: Cache CatalogDB or skip _create_schema on repeated get_db() calls
tasks.description:
Every call to get_db() in autopilot/web/deps.py constructs a new CatalogDB instance, which opens a SQLite connection, fires two PRAGMAs (journal_mode=WAL, foreign_keys=ON), and runs _create_schema() — an executescript with 19 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements. This overhead is paid on every HTTP request.

Now that get_db() is the single canonical entry point (after task 137 consolidated duplicates), this is the right place to optimize. Options:
1. Store a shared CatalogDB instance on app.state at startup and have get_db() return it (CatalogDB already uses check_same_thread=False and WAL mode).
2. At minimum, move _create_schema() behind a once-flag so the 19 DDL statements don't re-execute every request.

Note: Prior decision accepted SSE long-lived DB connection as-is for single-user localhost, so this is low priority but still worth doing for correctness.

tasks.title: Improve RoutingError specificity for file_path resolution edge cases
tasks.description:
Three related improvements to source_path resolution error handling in autopilot/render/router.py and its tests:

1. **Add explicit-None edge case test**: `test_missing_file_path_key_raises_routing_error` covers `db.get_media.return_value = {}` (missing key) but not `{"file_path": None}` (explicit null). Add a companion test `test_explicit_null_file_path_raises_routing_error` that sets `db.get_media.return_value = {"file_path": None}` and asserts RoutingError is raised. Both cases hit the same `media.get("file_path") is None` guard at router.py:145-148.

2. **Tighten assertion match pattern**: In `test_missing_file_path_key_raises_routing_error` (line 1108), change `match="file_path"` to `match=r"has no file_path"` to avoid false matches against the "No media record" RoutingError.

3. **Distinguish missing-key vs None-value in error message** (router.py:145-149): Replace the single check with:
   ```python
   if "file_path" not in media:
       raise RoutingError(f"file_path key missing from media record for clip {clip_id!r}")
   if media["file_path"] is None:
       raise RoutingError(f"file_path is null in media record for clip {clip_id!r}")
   ```
   This aids debugging when the root cause differs (DB schema bug vs intentional null).

tasks.title: Template JS quality: hoist parseFloat and add isFinite guard
tasks.description:
In `autopilot/web/templates/partials/narrative_edit_form.html` line ~24, refactor the hx-vals IIFE from:
`(v => v !== '' && !isNaN(parseFloat(v)) ? parseFloat(v) : null)(...)`
to:
`(v => { const n = parseFloat(v); return v !== '' && !isNaN(n) && isFinite(n) ? n : null })(...)`
This eliminates the redundant double `parseFloat` call and adds an `isFinite` guard to prevent `Infinity` (e.g. from `1e308`) from being silently coerced to `null` by `JSON.stringify`.

tasks.title: Harden prune_events() guard: reject non-integer hours and align ValueError style
tasks.description:
Two small fixes in autopilot/db.py prune_events() (around line 1467):

1. **Float rejection**: The current guard `if hours <= 0` doesn't catch non-integer values like `hours=0.5`. SQLite's `datetime('now', '-0.5 hours')` returns NULL, making the WHERE clause always-false — zero rows deleted with no error. Add `isinstance(hours, int)` check.

2. **ValueError style**: The inline `raise ValueError(f"hours must be positive, got {hours}")` doesn't follow the local convention used in all 5 other ValueError raises in db.py (lines 782, 866, 1303, 1387, 1530), which assign to `msg` first. Align with: `msg = f"hours must be positive, got {hours}"; raise ValueError(msg)`.

Suggested fix:
```python
if not isinstance(hours, int) or hours <= 0:
    msg = f"hours must be a positive integer, got {hours!r}"
    raise ValueError(msg)
```

Update tests in tests/test_db_pipeline.py to add a case for float input (e.g., `hours=0.5`).

- search: "db.py ValueError msg variable pattern convention"
- search: "prune_events guard validation"

tasks.title: Fix false subsumption claim and tighten assertions in zero-duration tests
tasks.description:
Three fixes in `tests/test_review.py` within the zero-duration test class:

1. **Restore slim `|| null` negative guard** (suggestions 1, 2, 4): The deleted `test_edit_form_js_does_not_use_falsy_or_null` test covered a real gap that the roundtrip test does NOT cover. The roundtrip test sends JSON directly via `put(..., json={...})` and never inspects the rendered hx-vals inline JS. Restore a slim test that GETs the edit form and asserts `'|| null' not in response.text` — this is a fast, isolated regression guard against the template being reverted from the safe `!isNaN(parseFloat(v))` pattern to the dangerous `v || null` pattern. Do NOT assert on `isNaN` (that was the implementation-detail assertion task 220 correctly removed).

2. **Fix misleading docstring** (suggestions 2, 4): In `test_update_zero_duration_roundtrip` (line ~1093-1098), the docstring says "This behavioural roundtrip also subsumes JS-pattern regression coverage" — this is factually wrong. The TestClient makes direct HTTP/JSON calls and never executes browser-side JS. Correct the docstring to accurately state it covers server-side persistence and rendering only.

3. **Tighten `value=""` assertion** (suggestions 3, 5): In `test_edit_form_renders_empty_for_none_duration` (line ~1087), `assert 'value=""' in response.text` could match any empty-value input on the page. Tie the assertion to the specific field, e.g. parse the HTML to find the input with `name="proposed_duration_seconds"` and check its value attribute, or use a regex/substring that combines field name and value in the same element.

tasks.title: Use mock.assert_not_called() with comment instead of assert not mock.called in test_cli.py
tasks.description:
Task 225 fixed the silent-tuple bug in assert_not_called() calls (lines 590, 693, 712, 731) by replacing them with `assert not mock.called, msg`. While correct, this deviates from the file's convention of using the mock assertion API (assert_called_once(), assert_called_once_with(), etc.) and loses Mock's built-in rich diagnostic output on failure.

Fix: Change the four sites back to `mock.assert_not_called()` as a standalone statement, and move the custom message to a comment on the same line. Example:
```python
stage_func.assert_not_called()  # --dry-run should NOT call any stage function
```

This preserves consistency with the rest of the test module while eliminating the original silent-tuple bug.

tasks.title: Standardize call_args access to .kwargs/.args across test suite
tasks.description:
Task 225 introduced `call_args.kwargs` at test_cli.py:791, but the rest of the test suite (50+ occurrences across 10 files) uses the legacy tuple-index style `call_args[1]`. This creates an inconsistent mix.

Migrate all `call_args[0]` → `call_args.args` and `call_args[1]` → `call_args.kwargs` across the test suite. The named-attribute form is more explicit, self-documenting, and robust (doesn't break if positional args are added). Available since Python 3.8.

Files to check: test_cli.py (lines 149, 187, 205, 510, 526, 791), test_orchestrator.py, test_gpu_scheduler.py, test_asr.py, and other test files using call_args tuple indexing.

tasks.title: Fix mock wrapping fidelity and coverage in test_prune_events_parameterizes_hours
tasks.description:
The current `patch.object(catalog_db, 'conn', wraps=catalog_db.conn)` wraps the connection object itself, but child attributes like `mock_conn.execute` become plain MagicMocks that do NOT forward calls to the real `conn.execute`. This means `prune_events()` no longer actually runs the DELETE against the in-memory DB during this test.

Fix by patching only the execute method: `patch.object(catalog_db.conn, 'execute', wraps=catalog_db.conn.execute) as mock_exec` and inspecting `mock_exec.call_args_list` directly. This preserves real DB I/O while still capturing call args.

Also:
1. Move all assertions inside the `with` block (conventional idiom, avoids readability confusion about mock lifecycle).
2. Add a second parametrized edge case for `hours=1` to verify parameterization works for the common default boundary value.

tasks.title: Harden idempotency-skip tests in test_audio_events.py
tasks.description:
Two minor issues in the idempotency-skip tests added by tasks 203 and 213:

1. **Remove trivially-true `scheduler.assert_not_called()` assertions.**
   `classify_audio_events` only calls `scheduler.model("panns_cnn14")` (audio_events.py line 125), never `scheduler(...)` directly. So `scheduler.assert_not_called()` is trivially true in all code paths and doesn't verify the skip behavior. The meaningful assertion is `scheduler.model.assert_not_called()`, which is already present.
   - Line ~365: `test_skip_when_events_exist_at_nonzero_timestamp`
   - Line ~400: `test_skip_when_events_exist_beyond_range`

2. **Guard against Path.exists ordering fragility.**
   Both skip tests pass `/tmp/vid1.wav` to `classify_audio_events` without patching `Path.exists`. This works only because the idempotency guard (line 95-98) fires before the file-existence check (line 101). If that ordering reverses, the test fails with `AudioEventError` instead of testing the regression.
   Either wrap the call with `patch.object(Path, 'exists', return_value=True)` (matching the pattern in `test_processes_when_no_events`), or add an inline comment explaining why the path doesn't need to exist.

tasks.title: Improve _setup_media_with_detections helper interface and fixture ordering
tasks.description:
In tests/test_youtube.py, the `_setup_media_with_detections` helper has two related issues:

1. **Leaky interface**: The `activity_cluster_ids_json` parameter accepts a pre-encoded JSON string, forcing callers to call `json.dumps()` at the call site. Rename to `activity_cluster_ids: list[str] | None` and do `json.dumps(value) if value is not None else None` inside the helper.

2. **Inverted fixture ordering**: In `test_tags_from_activity_labels_and_detections` (line ~219), `insert_activity_cluster("c1", ...)` and `insert_activity_cluster("c2", ...)` are called *after* `_setup_media_with_detections` which inserts a narrative referencing those clusters. Although SQLite doesn't enforce FK constraints on JSON columns, the ordering is conceptually inverted. Move `insert_activity_cluster` calls before the helper call (or into the helper when cluster IDs are provided), and add a brief comment if ordering still matters.

Memory hints: search "task 202 _setup_media_with_detections helper rename" for prior context.

tasks.title: Add edge-case test for dangling activity cluster references in _build_upload_metadata
tasks.description:
In tests/test_youtube.py, there is no test covering the case where `activity_cluster_ids_json` in a narrative references cluster IDs that don't exist in the DB (stale/dangling references). The existing `test_tags_from_activity_labels_and_detections` always inserts matching clusters.

Add a test like `test_tags_empty_when_activity_clusters_missing` that:
1. Calls `_setup_media_with_detections` with `activity_cluster_ids_json=json.dumps(["nonexistent"])` (or the updated `activity_cluster_ids=["nonexistent"]` parameter if the interface improvement task lands first)
2. Does NOT call `insert_activity_cluster`
3. Asserts `_build_upload_metadata` produces no activity-label tags (graceful handling, not an error)

Memory hints: search "task 202 _setup_media_with_detections" for helper context.

tasks.title: Adopt soft-assertion pattern in TestDryRunZeroSideEffects
tasks.description:
In tests/test_cli.py, the consolidated `test_dry_run_no_side_effects` method (class TestDryRunZeroSideEffects) uses three sequential `assert not mock.called` checks. If the first assertion fails, pytest stops and the remaining two are never evaluated, masking whether CatalogDB or PipelineOrchestrator were also incorrectly called.

Replace the sequential assertions with a failure-collector pattern that evaluates all three conditions and reports all failures in a single AssertionError:

```python
failures = []
if mock_load.called:
    failures.append(f'{cmd_name} --dry-run should NOT call load_config')
if mock_db_cls.called:
    failures.append(f'{cmd_name} --dry-run should NOT instantiate CatalogDB')
if mock_orch_cls.called:
    failures.append(f'{cmd_name} --dry-run should NOT instantiate PipelineOrchestrator')
assert not failures, '\n'.join(failures)
```

Memory hints: search for "TestDryRunZeroSideEffects consolidation" and "task 226 test consolidation".

tasks.title: Add 'planned' status branch to narrative_status_badge macro
tasks.description:
The status_badge macro in autopilot/web/templates/macros/narrative_status_badge.html explicitly maps 4 statuses (proposed, approved, rejected, scripted) but the system has a 5th known lifecycle status: 'planned' (set after EDL generation). It currently falls through to the purple 'unknown' fallback, giving no visual distinction between a legitimate 'planned' state and a truly unknown/bad status.

**Changes needed:**
1. Add an `elif status == 'planned'` branch with a semantically distinct color (e.g., `bg-indigo-900 text-indigo-300`) before the else clause in `autopilot/web/templates/macros/narrative_status_badge.html`.
2. Add a `test_planned_indigo` test in `TestStatusBadgeMacro` in `tests/test_narrative_status_badge.py` that renders with status='planned' and asserts the correct CSS classes.

**Memory hints:** search for "narrative lifecycle statuses" and "planned status" to confirm the full set of statuses.

tasks.description:
The test classes TestReviewScriptsBadge and TestReviewNarrativesBadge in tests/test_narrative_status_badge.py only perform static source-text checks (verifying the import line exists). They do not render the templates or verify the macro is actually invoked. Several improvements are needed:

1. **Parametrize source checks** (lines 130-145): TestReviewScriptsBadge and TestReviewNarrativesBadge are structurally identical — same assertion, only the file path differs. Replace both classes with a single parametrized test: `@pytest.mark.parametrize('template', ['review/narratives.html', 'review/scripts.html'])` to avoid spawning a new class per template.

2. **Add `status_badge(` call assertion** (lines 142-145): The current source check only asserts the import line exists, not that `status_badge(...)` is actually called. Add `assert 'status_badge(' in source` alongside the import assertion.

3. **Add render-level tests for review templates**: Add end-to-end render tests similar to TestNarrativeCardBadge.test_renders_approved_badge — construct a minimal context, render the template, and assert expected CSS classes appear. This would catch regressions if the macro call is removed. Note: the old inline badge in scripts.html used gray fallback colors, but the macro uses purple — this behavioral change is currently unverified.

**Files to modify:**
- tests/test_narrative_status_badge.py

queries: status_badge macro render-level tests template
review narratives scripts badge import usage
narrative_status_badge wrapper pattern test quality

tasks.title: Strengthen _execute_kwargs_update test coverage
tasks.description:
Add two missing test scenarios to `TestExecuteKwargsUpdate` in `tests/test_db_validate_helper.py`:

1. **PK-not-found test**: Call `_execute_kwargs_update` with non-empty kwargs and a pk_val that was never inserted. Assert return value is 0 and no exception is raised. The docstring promises "0 when the primary-key value is not found" but this is never tested.

2. **Unchanged column assertion**: In `test_executes_update_and_returns_rowcount`, after updating `label` and `description`, also assert that at least one other column (e.g. `location_label`) remains unchanged. Currently a regression that zeroed out non-updated columns would go undetected.

Example for PK-not-found:
```python
def test_returns_zero_when_pk_not_found(self, catalog_db):
    rowcount = catalog_db._execute_kwargs_update(
        "activity_clusters", "cluster_id", "does_not_exist",
        frozenset({"label"}), "cluster", {"label": "X"},
    )
    assert rowcount == 0
```

tasks.title: Add identifier validation for table/pk_col in _execute_kwargs_update

Add assertions at the top of `_execute_kwargs_update`:
```python
assert table.isidentifier(), f"Invalid table name: {table}"
assert pk_col.isidentifier(), f"Invalid pk_col: {pk_col}"
```

Alternatively, define a private allowlist of valid (table, pk_col) pairs. The `noqa: S608` comment claims "column names validated against allowlist" but that only covers SET-clause keys via `_validate_update_kwargs`, not table/pk_col.

tasks.title: Clarify or-empty vs is-not-none Jinja convention for nullable string fields
tasks.description:
Resolve idiom divergence in Jinja templates for nullable field handling:

The edit form (narrative_edit_form.html) now uses `{{ x if x is not none else '' }}` for string fields (title, description), while 9 other templates use `{{ x or '' }}`. For string fields, `or ''` is functionally identical and is the established project convention.

Recommended resolution:
- Revert narrative_edit_form.html lines 4 and 11 to `{{ narrative.title or '' }}` / `{{ narrative.description or '' }}` (consistent with the 9-site project convention).
- Add a comment on the proposed_duration_seconds field explaining why `is not none` is required there (numeric field where 0 is meaningful).
- This satisfies task 223's comment-or-standardize requirement without introducing inconsistency.

Reference templates using `or ''`: narrative_card.html:7, cluster_card.html:14, review/narratives.html:29, media/list.html:24, and others.

Memory hints: search "zero-value falsy handling Jinja" for the convention from task 167; search "or '' Jinja template convention" for related norms.

tasks.title: Fix fragile hours=1 assertion and simplify parametrize in test_prune_events_parameterizes_hours
tasks.description:
In `test_prune_events_parameterizes_hours`, the assertion `str(hours) not in sql_arg` is fragile for `hours=1` because the single digit "1" commonly appears in SQL strings (e.g., `LIMIT 1`, version literals). Any future SQL change could cause a spurious failure.

Fix:
1. Replace `hours=1` with a less collision-prone value like `hours=7` or drop the `str(hours) not in sql_arg` assertion entirely (the parameter-equality assertion already covers the parameterization contract).
2. Drop the `expected_param` column from the parametrize decorator — it's derivable from `hours` via `f"-{hours} hours"`, creating a maintenance hazard. Simplify to `@pytest.mark.parametrize("hours", [48, 7])` and compute expected inline.

memory_hints: search "test_prune_events fragile assertion" and "task 245 review suggestions"

tasks.title: Broaden mock convention guard to cover all test files and additional patterns
tasks.description:
The convention guard in tests/test_cli_conventions.py currently only scans test_cli.py. Three improvements needed:

1. **Scan all test files**: Change from hard-coded `test_cli.py` to iterating `Path(__file__).parent.glob('test_*.py')` (excluding the convention file itself). The project has 60+ test files that could independently reintroduce the anti-pattern.

2. **Handle dotted mock names**: The regex `r"assert\s+not\s+\w+\.called\b"` misses chained attribute patterns like `self.mock.called` or `mock_obj.child.called`. Replace `\w+` with `[\w.]+` to match dotted attribute chains.

3. **Guard positive pattern too**: Add a second regex to catch `assert mock.called` (without `not`) which should use `mock.assert_called()` or `mock.assert_called_once()` instead.

4. **Fix misleading docstring**: The module docstring incorrectly states the old pattern "silently swallows custom messages when written as tuples." The actual motivation is that `mock.assert_not_called()` is the idiomatic Mock API producing richer failure output (call count, args). Update accordingly.

Change to something like "Scheduler model should NOT be loaded (scheduler.model is the only entry point)" or add an inline comment explaining `scheduler()` is never called directly, only `scheduler.model(...)`.

3. **Add two defensive tests**:
   a. A test proving idempotency short-circuits even when the file does NOT exist: pre-insert events, call with nonexistent path (no Path.exists patch), assert no exception and scheduler.model not called. This guards against future reordering of the idempotency check vs path check.
   b. A test for the AudioEventError path: no prior events in DB, call with nonexistent path, assert `AudioEventError` is raised. This covers audio_events.py:101-102 which is currently untested.

tasks.title: Centralize narrative status definitions and color mappings
tasks.description:
Narrative status values (proposed, approved, rejected, scripted, planned, sourced) and their Tailwind color class mappings are scattered across multiple independent locations with no single source of truth:
- Database schema (db.py)
- Template macro (narrative_status_badge.html)
- Pipeline orchestrator (orchestrator.py)
- EDL planner (plan/edl.py)
- Source resolver (source/resolve.py)
- Duplicate `_STATUS_COLORS` dicts in pipeline.py and dashboard.py
- `_GATE_STATUS_COLORS` in gates.py also inlined in gate_toggle.html
- Ad-hoc inline mappings in media_row.html

Adding a new status requires manual updates to every location.

Changes needed:
1. Define a `NarrativeStatus` enum or string constants in a shared module (e.g., `autopilot/constants.py` or `autopilot/models.py`).
2. Extract narrative status→Tailwind class mappings into a shared Python constant (e.g., `STATUS_BADGE_CLASSES` in `autopilot/web/colors.py` or similar).
3. Inject the mapping into the Jinja2 environment as a global so the macro can reference it.
4. Update all status-aware locations to import from the shared module.
5. Update tests to verify consistency.

tasks.title: Add test for graceful degradation with missing activity clusters
tasks.description:
In tests/test_youtube.py, there is no test covering what `_build_upload_metadata` returns when `activity_cluster_ids_json` references cluster IDs that do not exist in the DB. Since `activity_cluster_ids_json` is a plain TEXT column with no FK constraint, a missing-cluster scenario silently succeeds at insert time but may produce empty/wrong tags at query time. This failure mode is reachable in production if cluster data is deleted or missing.

Add a test like:
```python
def test_tags_missing_activity_clusters_gracefully_degrades(self, catalog_db, youtube_config):
    """Tags fall back to detection-only values when referenced clusters don't exist."""
    self._setup_media_with_detections(
        catalog_db,
        [("m1", 0, json.dumps([{"class": "person", "confidence": 0.9}]))],
        activity_cluster_ids=["nonexistent_c1"],
    )
    meta = _build_upload_metadata("n1", catalog_db, youtube_config)
    tags = meta["snippet"]["tags"]
    # Should not raise; detection tags should still be present
    assert "person" in tags
```

Memory hints: search "activity_cluster_ids _build_upload_metadata tags" for implementation details.

```python
def _assert_warning_contains(caplog, substring):
    msgs = [r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records if r.levelno == logging.WARNING]
    assert any(substring in m for m in msgs), (
        f"Expected a WARNING containing {substring!r}, got: {msgs}"
    )
```

tasks.title: Fix vacuous and misleading assertions in TestSettingsPage
tasks.description: In tests/test_settings.py, three test methods have assertions that pass vacuously because they check strings present on every page via base.html's nav bar. Also consolidate redundant HTTP calls.
tasks.details:
Fix these specific issues in tests/test_settings.py:

1. **test_settings_contains_page_title (line 17):** `assert 'Settings' in response.text` passes vacuously because 'Settings' appears in the nav on every page. Change to `assert '<title>Settings - Autopilot Video</title>' in response.text`.

2. **test_settings_extends_base_template (line 32):** Asserts 'Dashboard' and 'Autopilot Video' which are nav-bar strings present on all pages. Assert a settings-specific string + a nav element together, or check the `<title>` tag.

3. **Missing coverage:** Add an assertion that `<title>Settings - Autopilot Video</title>` appears in the rendered HTML to catch regressions in the page_title context variable.

4. **Redundant IO:** Convert the 5 independent `client.get('/settings')` calls in TestSettingsPage into a single class-scoped response fixture to reduce round-trips.

tasks.title: Harden TestNavLinkContract: status codes, nav parsing, and input guards
tasks.description: TestNavLinkContract in tests/test_settings.py has several defensive gaps: it's in the wrong file, only catches 404s (not 500s), uses a fragile regex for nav parsing, lacks an existence check on base.html, and doesn't filter external URLs.
tasks.details:
Fix these specific issues (currently in tests/test_settings.py, TestNavLinkContract class):

1. **Move to dedicated file:** Move TestNavLinkContract to `tests/test_navigation.py` — it tests all nav links in base.html, not just settings. Its current placement is misleading.

2. **Catch all error status codes (line 70):** Change `if response.status_code == 404` to `if response.status_code >= 400` to catch 500s from broken route handlers.

3. **Fix fragile nav regex (lines 56/63):** Replace `re.search(r'<nav\b[^>]*>(.*?)</nav>', html, re.DOTALL)` — it silently truncates on nested or secondary nav elements. Use html.parser or re.findall over all nav blocks.

4. **Add existence check (line 53):** Add `assert base_html.exists(), f'base.html not found at {base_html}'` before read_text() for a self-documenting failure message.

5. **Filter external URLs (line 64):** Filter hrefs to relative URLs only (`[h for h in hrefs if h.startswith('/')]`) before iterating, to prevent TestClient attempting real outbound HTTP requests on external links.

## Memory hints
- search: "TestNavLinkContract nav link test"
- search: "base.html nav structure"

tasks.title: Fix conflicting Tailwind utility classes in status_badge macro
tasks.description:
The status_badge macro at autopilot/web/templates/macros/status_badge.html:2 hardcodes px-2, py-1, rounded, text-xs, and font-medium unconditionally. This causes silent visual bugs: (1) media/detail.html:16 and media_row.html:23 pass extra_classes='py-0.5', producing duplicate py-1+py-0.5 where Tailwind's stylesheet order (not HTML order) determines the winner; (2) renders.html:39 passes extra_classes='inline-flex items-center px-3 rounded-full text-sm', conflicting with px-2, rounded, and text-xs; (3) font-medium is added to stage badges that originally had none, changing visual weight; (4) when extra_classes is empty the class string has a redundant double space. Fix: redesign the macro to accept named override parameters (px, py, rounded, text_size, font_weight) or strip conflicting base classes and require callers to supply all layout/spacing via extra_classes. Update tests in tests/test_status_badge.py to assert the correct non-conflicting class strings are rendered.
